title: Aarti (Waving of the Lamp)
summary: Waving a lit lamp in a clockwise circle before the deity at the end of puja. Brief, universal, and performed in nearly every Hindu tradition.

steps.title: Prepare the lamp
steps.instruction: Use a camphor or ghee lamp with multiple wicks (usually 1, 3, 5, or 9). Light just before the aarti — it should not have been burning for a long time beforehand.
materials: camphor or ghee with cotton wicks

steps.title: Sing or recite the aarti
steps.instruction: Stand before the deity. Sing the aarti song appropriate to the deity (e.g., 'Om Jai Jagadish Hare' for a general Vaishnava context; 'Karpoora Gauram' for Shiva; 'Jai Ambe Gauri' for Devi). A ring of a small bell accompanies the singing.

steps.title: Wave the lamp
steps.instruction: Move the lamp clockwise in a vertical circle before the deity — first at the feet, then the chest, then the face, then the whole form. Five rotations is common.

steps.title: Share the flame
steps.instruction: Offer the flame to gathered devotees. Each person cups their hands over the flame briefly and touches the warmth to their forehead and eyes as a gesture of receiving grace.

steps.title: Extinguish and close
steps.instruction: Extinguish the lamp (do not blow it out — use a smothering motion or let the camphor burn out). Sprinkle tirtha water and distribute prasad.
